name: Marinated Spicy Jerky- Thai Style
recipeIngredient: 8 pounds Beef or caribou round or flank steak
4 Scallions, sliced in half into 1/4" lengths
2 Bird or other fresh hot chiles, seeded & finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim all fat off the meat and cut against the grain into 1/4" thick strips.
Meat is easier to cut when partially frozen and it will cut evenly.
2. In a saucepan, combine beef stock, lime juice, fish sauce, sugar; bring to a boil over high heat.
3. Add mint, shallots, scallions, and chiles. Cool and strain the mixture.
4. Mix the strained stock and remaining marinade ingredients in a bowl.
5. Add the meat and cover. Marinade overnight. Alternatively, put meat and marinade in a sealable plastic bag.
6. Turn the meat occasionally to ensure all portions get well soaked in the marinade.
7. Remove from marinade and let dry on a rack.
Line a cookie sheet with foil and arrange meat in a single layer, or place meat directly on oven racks, line the bottom of the oven with foil, or on a rack in a shallow pan.
8. Dry for 6 hours or more at 175°F, turning after 3 hours.
Continue to dry in a warm oven if necessary. Gas ovens with pilot lights work especially well.
9. Cool and bag it.
